issues.title: Form validation error display missing
issues.attack: org_info.html and org_invites.html render form widgets but no field.errors or non_field_errors. Invalid submissions re-render silently with no explanation.
issues.response: Valid. No existing templates use field.errors but this is a genuine gap. Will add per-field errors and non_field_errors to both forms.

issues.title: Tab bar uses buttons instead of links (no fallback)
issues.attack: Tab bar uses <button hx-get> without <a href> fallback. Mutation forms lack action attributes. If HTMX fails, tabs and actions break.
issues.response: Existing settings_tab_bar.html uses identical <button hx-get> pattern. HTMX is a hard dependency, not optional. Changing only org tabs would break consistency.

issues.title: Verification step lacks HTMX rendering tests
issues.attack: Step 6 only runs status code/DB tests. No HTMX rendering tests for full-shell vs partial, tab bar presence. Known regressions from t14/t15 could recur.
issues.response: Valid. Will add HTMX rendering tests mirroring test_settings_tabs.py: full-page, main entry, tab switch.

issues.title: org_info.html missing success message display
issues.attack: View passes message in context but template has no {% if message %} block, unlike org_members and org_invites.
issues.response: Clear oversight. Will add message display consistent with other partials.

issues.title: Inconsistent feedback mechanism (static msg vs toast)
issues.attack: Static message div and JavaScript toast used inconsistently. Long lists hide success message at top of re-rendered content.
issues.response: Switching to HX-Trigger toast requires views_org.py changes — out of t16 template-only scope. Static message consumes view context. Toast for clipboard is inherently client-side.

issues.title: Clipboard copy error handling
issues.attack: navigator.clipboard.writeText() is async but toast fires synchronously. Failed copy still shows success.
issues.response: Accept Promise chaining fix. Maintain MINOR severity — HTTPS production + localhost both support clipboard API.
